channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 14 things keeping IT leaders up at night
item.description: There’s a lot for CIOs to stress about these days — from longstanding concerns about uptime and availability to the quantum revolution on the horizon. Of course, there are plenty of other issues worrying CIOs here and now, too.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: MIT and Generali Form Strategic Partnership to Explore AI Applications
item.description: MIT and Generali form strategic partnership to explore AI applications in insurance sector
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Data Privacy Week 2025 has begun – check out our latest expert advice on how to stay safe online
item.description: Data Privacy Week 2025 is all about taking control of your data. On this page, we put together all the best explainers, tips, and tools to help you exactly do that.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: SD-WAN to gain AI-driven deployment, management capabilities
item.description: AI is set to make its mark on SD-WAN technology. For starters, generative AI capabilities will improve how enterprise IT teams deploy and manage their SD-WAN architecture. In addition, AI workloads have distinctive requirements that will influence SD-WAN connectivity choices.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Connected Car: A Data Privacy Nightmare - Cybersecurity Risks Exposed
item.description: The connected car poses a serious security risk, with vulnerabilities allowing hackers to remotely access and control vehicles. Subaru's Starlink-connected infotainment system has been exposed as having critical flaws.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New bid for TikTok from Perplexity AI could give US government 50% stake
item.description: The proposal is a revision of a prior plan the artificial intelligence startup had presented to TikTok’s parent ByteDance a day before the law that bans TikTok went into effect.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Don’t let this credit card fraud nightmare happen to you
item.description: Over 400,000 cases of credit card fraud were reported in 2024 to the Federal Trade Commission. The CyberGuy explains how to protect against becoming another victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Presumed Innocent season 2: everything we know so far about the Apple TV Plus show's return
item.description: Here's the latest news and rumors surrounding Presumed Innocent season 2, which will air on Apple TV Plus.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Musk vs. Altman
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Did you show ‘negative sentiment’ for insurance companies after the UHC CEO shooting? Police were watching
item.description: When news broke that the United Healthcare CEO was shot in broad daylight early last month, outrage erupted online. But it wasn’t aimed at the assassin. Instead, it was directed at the broken U.S. healthcare system he represented.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Meta to invest up to $65B in AI infrastructure, develop an ‘AI engineer’ agent
item.description: Not to be outdone by its close rival OpenAI, Meta has announced its plans to spend $60 to $65 billion on AI infrastructure this year, and is building a data center almost as big as Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DOJ indicts North Korean conspirators for remote IT work scheme
item.description: The US Department of Justice this week announced that it had indicted two North Korean nationals and three other men, accusing them of participating in a conspiracy designed to trick US companies into funding the North Korean regime.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Planted envelope, pilfered identities, money that didn’t exist: Bay Area couple accused of $60 million AI startup fraud
item.description: The indictment describes an enterprise constantly teetering on the edge of failure as the Beckmans allegedly solicited and obtained investments via false financial documents that dramatically overstated company sales, profit and bank balances.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Modernizing bp’s application landscape with AI
item.description: One of the oldest and largest oil and gas companies in the world, bp is in the midst of a major transition as it pivots toward becoming an integrated energy company. As part of this transition, the company is aiming for a net-zero carbon footprint by 2050.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Data center growth puts unprecedented pressure on power grids
item.description: According to a report released this week by Bloom Energy, US data centers will need 55 gigawatts of new power capacity within the next five years. The report, based on a survey of 100 data center leaders, also shows that 30% of all sites will be using onsite power by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: FortiGate admins advised to run a compromise assessment
item.description: Network administrators with Fortinet’s FortiGate next generation firewall in their IT environments are being warned to thoroughly scrutinize systems for possible compromise, following last week’s dump of stolen configuration and VPN credentials by a threat actor.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Ready or not, here it comes: GenAI in 2025
item.description: If you’re a software developer right now, it is nearly impossible to avoid chatter about generative AI. You’re probably already using AI tools in your work, whether you like it or not. Some of your normie friends are using ChatGPT as a search engine, with hilarious and alarming results.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JetBrains launches AI coding agent
item.description: JetBrains has launched an AI coding agent, named Junie, with the ability to quickly solve coding tasks that otherwise could take hours, according to JetBrains.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI’s new Operator is a step into AI’s agentic future
item.description: OpenAI announced on Thursday a research preview of Operator, an AI agent that can browse the web and perform tasks for the user. Operator is powered by the Computer-Using Agent (CUA), an AI model that merges GPT-4o’s vision capabilities with reasoning capability.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: A new Instagram feature might expose your embarrassing habits
item.description: Instagram Reels has added a new feature that shows you a feed of videos that your friends have liked. The bad news: It works both ways, meaning your friends can now see every video you’ve liked.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: The Oval Office ‘Stargate Project’ reveal was just more tech industry genuflecting
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Massachusetts Democrats ask regulators to open Trump meme coins probe
item.description: Two Massachusetts Democrats sent a letter to various regulators asking them to open an investigation into President Trump's and first lady Melania Trump's recently launched meme coins. The letter, signed by Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) and Rep. Jake Auchincloss (D-Mass.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Nearly 250 million children missed school last year because of extreme weather, UNICEF says
item.description: The United Nations Children's Fund says at least 242 million children in 85 countries had their schooling interrupted last year because of heatwaves, cyclones, flooding and other extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he will use energy emergency declaration to approve more power for AI
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that he will use his energy emergency declaration to approve power stations for artificial intelligence (AI) centers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: 3 hidden benefits of Dedicated Internet Access for enterprises
item.description: Reliable Internet connectivity is crucial for businesses to function efficiently and remain competitive in today’s digital world. While standard broadband connections may work for basic tasks, they often struggle to meet the demands of modern businesses.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Palo Alto Networks releases QRNG API framework
item.description: Cybersecurity company Palo Alto Networks has released the Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) Open API framework, enabling users to prepare for quantum security threats.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Race is on to finish San Jose hotel in time for major Nvidia conference
item.description: Developers hope to gallop to the finish line to complete a San Jose hotel in time for a big Nvidia conference downtown.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: What to do when you can’t escape your old boss on social media
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: The OpenJDK’s plans for Java in 2025
item.description: Java enhancement plans for 2025 range from improving performance in the foreign function and memory (FFM) API, to working on ahead-of-time (AOT) code compilation, to finalizing the structured concurrency API, according to a recently published presentation from the OpenJDK community.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Stargate Project launched for OpenAI AI infrastructure
item.description: In a venture unveiled by Pres. Donald Trump on January 21, companies including SoftBank, Oracle, OpenAI, and MGX are funding a new AI company, called the Stargate Project, to invest $500 billion to build AI infrastructure for OpenAI in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
